title: Correct Cannula Insertion Technique. The best method to insert a cannula.
filename: Correct Cannula Insertion Technique. The best method to insert a cannula..en.vtt
excerpt: Want to get something off my chest which is bad cannula techniques for some reason I see a lot of people doing additional quite painful steps while inserting a cannula that I just don't think you need so let me talk you through what I do which I think is more comfortable with pat…

segments.text: want to get something off my chest which is bad cannula techniques for some reason I see a lot of people doing additional quite painful steps while inserting a cannula that I just don't think you need so let me talk you through what I do which I think is more comfortable with patients and works just as well so first of all remember that your pilot hole is literally the track you want your cannula to go down so when you create your pilot hole it needs to be congruent with the direction that your cannula needs to go there's no point just making a hole and then putting your candidate in at a different angle so get that clear in your mind before you start the process the next thing is remember that the needle itself only has to go through the thicker connective tissue you only need to puncture the dermis you don't need to go all the way down the whole track that you want to stick the cannula because once you're into hypodermic fat it pushes the fat out the way anyway so deep insertions with a pilot hole needle are unnecessary and will cause bruising for no additional benefit make sure you use a needle that is equal or slightly bigger than the cannula it can be equal

because the skin stretches a little bit slightly bigger makes it a little bit easier but you don't need a big gaping hole in order to slide a cannula in which is the next step which often triggers a bit of discomfort in me when I watch it so if you're creating a pilot hole we only really want to insert approximately a third of the needle in the direction that we want to go so here I am demonstrating how I might approach treating a tear trough so we would slide the needle in just so that it breaks through the dermis I'm not going all the way in into these other areas where I could easily puncture a vessel or a nerve and cause unnecessary trauma I'm just going in about a third of the needle and then I'll stop I'll come out I'll remember the direction I was going in and I just come straight out I don't do what I see a lot of other people do which is this additional step where they think they're making the whole bigger and making the insertion of the cannula easier one of the common things is the stirring technique so if you think about what's going on if you stir in this position you're dragging a sharp tip around in the fat or perhaps in the

muscle but the actual hole that you need to get through is surrounded by the non-sharp of the needle so it's actually not getting any bigger At All by doing that you're simply just dragging a sharp tip around above some of the structures causing unnecessary trauma the next thing I see many injectors do is they drag the needle out and flick it now to be fair I was taught this way and I have done it in the past but I certainly don't think it's necessary to do it anymore so as you drag the needle out you purposely drag the sharp bit of the needle to cut as if it's a cannula through the skin so that you make the slip bigger it's not needed you don't need to do that in order to get a cannula in to the skin now the reason you don't need those additional steps is because of how you put the cannula into the dermis once you're ready we don't simply jab until it goes in this is a common thing I see the sort of jabbing hoping it'll hit the right spot and it might eventually hit the right spot but you're too often are pushing again against dermis that won't let you in and if you just put push harder you eventually cause trauma through essentially using what's a very big blunt needle to force its way into the skin so what we want to find is the easy

access route which I sometimes describe as a bit like using a stick if you're a blind person trying to find a space you would tap gently around the hole waiting for it to slide in this works very well if there's a tiny drop of blood or some lubrication it slides along the surface and when it's in the right space it will slip into the hole and that's usually the hardest bit getting through the dermis now remember we want to be angled in the way that we initially created the pilot hole which should be in alignment with where we're going so now I've slid straight through the dermis the tip of my cannula should be in the hypodermic fat at which point I can change my angle of insertion and now I'm staying in a single layer as I pass my cannula gently up I like to be very gentle if you're rough and you tear through tissues instead of allow them to be pushed out the way gently you negate many of the benefits of a cannula so I'm gently feeling my way now looking for an easy passage and I've changed directions in multiple ways so I'll sometimes push a little bit up a little bit down left right until a track is found now

remember what's going on underneath the skin is occasionally you're up against tissue if you're trying to get through layers of tissue and it's blocking like this if you imagine my fingers are strands of tissue that block the cannula's passageway we're trying to find a way through if you don't come out far enough you just hold on to the tissue that's blocking it it's simply just moves with the cannula with your cannula underneath the tissue and it's not passing it's often attached to or at least stuck against some sort of connective tissue so if you don't pull the cannula out and you simply try and move the tip I think what you're doing often is dragging tissue with your tip so it never really will find a way past this is why you need to come out then change angle and then try and find a Passage through the tissue that's really what we're trying to do when you're finding these spaces you have to come out before you go back in staying at the same depth and looking to go left and right doesn't always move anything except a bit of tissue that's blocking your path so we've come out we've tried different entry points high and low until we find a passageway but if that doesn't work the next thing you

can do is you can try and create a bit of negative pressure in the tissue or a bit of space by picking up the skin from above and basically lifting it so if you were to lift the tissue slightly you may create a few more spaces and then you've got another chance of finding your way through and another very useful sometimes semi-magical trick is to use a tiny blob of filler so this in this case similar to what I've already discussed if you come out then do a little blob of filler and then slide in the filler often opens up a space that then allows the cannula to slide through so I would find the area that I couldn't pass push against it come back to neutral do a squirt of filler which I'm hoping opens up the tissues come out slightly and then try and slide back in and more often than not it actually allows you a passageway that enables you to continue your journey we're doing that process of gently finding different angles trying a little bit of negative pressure creating a bit of room or opening up the tissue with a tiny drop of filler until we've reached all the way to the end point

where we want to go where you can then start to lay down filler gently retrograde or integrate as you're injecting that it's often appropriate to do both so you're gently filling as you go and this opens up the tissue and it usually gets easier as you start so the hardest part is the first third and once you're in the right place more often than not it happens it flows quite easily once you've done all these other steps and doing all of those things together with some patients usually dramatically reduces the level of discomfort and bruising that you might get if you use some of the more rough techniques if you'd like more practical videos like this don't forget to like And subscribe ring the notification Bell so that you see them when they appear
